title: euphorbia arida subsp. arida
intro: Euphorbia arida subsp. arida, also known as the desert spoon, is a succulent plant species native to the arid regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Euphorbia arida subsp. arida include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's milky sap can be extracted to create tinctures and salves for topical use, with the dried roots and leaves also being used in teas and infusions for internal consumption.
